q: What functional group, noted for its versatility in natural products, pharmaceuticals, and synthetic intermediates, serves as a key synthetic handle for transformations into a compound that is constructed using aromatic boronic acids in the Petasis boron–Mannich reaction?
path:
INTRODUCTION 
The nitrile moiety, a cornerstone of modern organic chemistry and material science, is integral 
to natural products, pharmaceuticals and synthetic intermediates due to its exceptional 
versatility and reactivity.16 It serves as a key synthetic handle for transformations into 
carboxylic acids, amines, and aldehydes, while also enhancing reactivity at neighboring sites 
of molecules.79 The hydrocyanation of readily available alkynes represents a straightforward 
httpsdoi.org10.26434chemrxiv2025qhvvj ORCID httpsorcid.org0000000347662135 Content not peerreviewed by ChemRxiv.
What functional group, noted for its versatility in natural products, pharmaceuticals, and synthetic intermediates, serves as a key synthetic handle for transformations into amines?

While these methods offer access to simple alkyl amines, they pose 
challenges in rapidly synthesizing more complex aliphatic amines and constructing amine libraries 
for structure activity relationship (SAR) studies12. In recent decades, the versatility of imines has 
led to a significant progress in the development of multicomponent reactions as flexible routes to 
amine synthesis13, 14, such as azaMoritaBaylisHillman15, Mannich16, Petasis boronMannich 
(PBM)17 and Strecker18 reacion. Among these, the threecomponent PBM reaction, first reported 
in 1993 19, has been an attractive method for medicinal chemists due to its ability to use widely 
available building blocks including amines, aldehydes, and aromatic boronic acids to rapidly 
access diverse C(sp2)rich chemical space17.
According to the text, which compound is synthesized using aromatic boronic acids as one of the building blocks in the Petasis boron–Mannich reaction?

c A fresh solution of TBADT 2.5 mol in 1.5 mL of the reaction mixture was added before the second

TBADTs wellknown propensity to abstract hydrogen atoms from CH3CN under the 
demanding conditions necessary to activate gaseous reagents.25 A gastoliquid ratio (GL) of 
201 (8 equiv.) was employed, with 2,6lutidine used to quench the hydrobromic acid formed 
during the reaction.
According to the described reaction setup, which compound is used to react with propane using a synergistic combination of a Ni complex and TBADT?

This leads to the hypothesis that the concentration of produced salicylic acid 
increases as temperature increases. Since temperature and rate of reaction have an exponential relationship, it would be 
expected for the increase in concentration of salicylic acid to occur exponentially as well (Arrhenius Kinetics Analysis, 
n.d.). Once salicylic acid, 763, is dissolved in water, it produces, with a 11 mole ratio, the salicylate dianion, 
7432, which is the conjugate base of salicylic acid. When iron (III) nitrate, (3)3, dissolves in water, it 
disassociates into 3 and 3 ions. The iron (III) ions then react with the water to produce the hexaaquairon 
(III) ion, (2)6
3, again with a 11 mole ratio.
Which compound, when dissolved in water, produces the salicylate dianion as described in the text?
